runtimeStatuses.measured-before-index-baseline: Allowed only when planner integrity passes and at least one exact-current-production query has a captured Hibernate SQL fingerprint.
runtimeStatuses.measured-diagnostic-not-production-basel: Clean reconstructed/synthetic diagnostic evidence that must not be used as a production before metric.
runtimeStatuses.invalid-pending-planner-integrity: Planner, analyze, n_mod_since_analyze, or boundary activity contamination; runner exits non-zero and adoption is forbidden.
runtimeStatuses.invalid-pending-start-integrity: Cross-artifact, anchor, source, schema, planner, or database preflight rejected the run before any EXPLAIN.
runtimeStatuses.invalid-pending-runtime-failure: A query-window monitor, schema/vacuum/source/artifact continuity, normalization, or runtime tool failure rejected a partial run.

measurementPhases.interpretation: The first sequential observation is not a cold cache measurement because shared PostgreSQL and OS caches are not reset.
Sequential repetitions after the first observation; compare only with the same dataset, fixture run, planner snapshot, and external-activity evidence.

plannerState.autoanalyzeComparison: Compare per-table analyze, autoanalyze, vacuum, autovacuum, nModSinceAnalyze, and visibility evidence between before and after snapshots.
plannerState.adoptionRule: Settings, schema/index fingerprint, and table statistics must be unchanged, with zero boundary or transient query-window external activity; otherwise adoption is blocked and the runner exits non-zero.

activityMonitor.scope: One continuous read-only observer covers every query and phase.
activityMonitor.databaseScope: Every non-idle client backend on the inspected PostgreSQL instance; only the observer PID and exact registered measured backend identities are excluded.
activityMonitor.samplingIntervalApproval: The user must approve and supply the positive integer for each measurement window; the scenario defines no methodology default.
activityMonitor.lifecycleRule: Application identity is prepared before psql spawn, then bound to the observed backend identity. Every expected label must be observed and explicitly unregistered; duplicate or incomplete registrations are invalid.
activityMonitor.contaminationRule: Transient external activity, including visibility-restricted client backends whose state is NULL, or observer exit notifies the runner immediately, aborts the in-flight measured psql, preserves a partial rejected report, and exits non-zero.
activityMonitor.shutdownRule: SIGTERM interrupts any remaining approved sampling delay so the worker can take its final sample and preserve the window report before the parent reap deadline.

evidenceClassification.productionBeforeRule: Only exact-current-production with a captured Hibernate SQL fingerprint may set productionBeforeEligible=true.
evidenceClassification.activationRule: A separate user-approved contract change and executable captured-SQL shape verification are required before production before evidence can be enabled.
